그래도 한번은 자동으로 잡겠지 하고 린트 걸고 고쳐봐했는데 못잡은 것은 한번 있었어요.
약간 무한 루프같은데 빠지더라고요
그나저나 회사에서는 인공지능은 그렇다 치는데 룰베이스 로봇들과도 싸워야 하는데...
contextW 같은 변수가 원래 있었는데 TW가 들어갔다 지역 기반의 코드가 들어간 것 같다. 아니면 상위 메니저에게 소명을 받아라 (...)
뭐 이런 일을 하고 있는데, 코드 래빗이나 제미나이가 얘네들보다는 낫죠..
(자바에서는 weak 란 의미로 뒤에 W를 붙이는게 컨벤션입니다.)
제가 짠것도 아닌데 (...)
그나저나 깃헙액션으로 해서 린트 에러만 체크해서 피드백을 줄 수 있나요? 워닝은 현재는 많아서 일단은 그대로 둬야 할 것 같아요.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- canvas-dev-tools: This skill provides Canvas LMS development utilities for testing LTI applications. It should be used when users need to create mock students with SIS IDs, accept course invitations in bulk, manage test enrollments, or perform other Canvas development tasks. All operations execute inline Ruby code via docker compose.
- dart-financial-analysis: This skill provides tools for analyzing South Korean corporate financial data from DART (Data Analysis, Retrieval and Transfer System). This skill should be used when users ask about Korean company financials, disclosures, business information, or want to compare financial metrics of Korean listed companies (KOSPI/KOSDAQ).
- template-skill: Replace with description of the skill and when Claude should use it.
- fedify-activitypub: Guide for building and maintaining ActivityPub/fediverse applications using Fedify. This skill should be used when users want to create federated social applications, implement ActivityPub protocols, build new features, test and debug federation, deploy to production, look up FEPs, or maintain existing Fedify applications.
- skill-share: A skill that creates new Claude skills and automatically shares them on Slack using Rube for seamless team collaboration and skill discovery.
- agent-creator: This skill should be used when the user asks to "create an agent", "write a subagent", "generate agent definition", "add agent to plugin", "write agent frontmatter", "create autonomous agent", "build subagent", needs agent structure guidance, YAML frontmatter configuration, invocation criteria with examples, or wants to add specialized subagents to Claude Code plugins with proper capabilities lists and tool access definitions.
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Claude's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.
- lsp-config: This skill should be used when the user wants to view, add, remove, or modify language server configurations in `.lsp.json`. It should be used when the user mentions `.lsp.json`, LSP config, language server setup, or wants to configure type-checking/linting tools for their project.
- obsidian:defuddle: Extract clean markdown content from web pages using Defuddle CLI, removing clutter and navigation to save tokens. Use instead of WebFetch when the user provides a URL to read or analyze, for online documentation, articles, blog posts, or any standard web page.
- obsidian:obsidian-cli: Interact with Obsidian vaults using the Obsidian CLI to read, create, search, and manage notes, tasks, properties, and more. Also supports plugin and theme development with commands to reload plugins, run JavaScript, capture errors, take screenshots, and inspect the DOM. Use when the user asks to interact with their Obsidian vault, manage notes, search vault content, perform vault operations from the command line, or develop and debug Obsidian plugins and themes.
- obsidian:json-canvas: Create and edit JSON Canvas files (.canvas) with nodes, edges, groups, and connections. Use when working with .canvas files, creating visual canvases, mind maps, flowcharts, or when the user mentions Canvas files in Obsidian.
- obsidian:obsidian-bases: Create and edit Obsidian Bases (.base files) with views, filters, formulas, and summaries. Use when working with .base files, creating database-like views of notes, or when the user mentions Bases, table views, card views, filters, or formulas in Obsidian.
- obsidian:obsidian-markdown: Create and edit Obsidian Flavored Markdown with wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, and other Obsidian-specific syntax. Use when working with .md files in Obsidian, or when the user mentions wikilinks, callouts, frontmatter, tags, embeds, or Obsidian notes.
- minimalism-workflow:ghpr: Create a GitHub pull request with auto-generated title and body from commits. Use when the user wants to create a PR, open a pull request, or says "ghpr".
- minimalism-workflow:gh-release-note: Generate a release changelog from git commits between the previous tag and HEAD, then create a GitHub release. This skill should be used when the user wants to write release notes, generate a changelog, create a GitHub release, or summarize what changed since the last release.
- minimalism-workflow:commit: Create a git commit with auto-generated message from staged changes. Use when the user wants to commit, create a commit, or says "commit".
- minimalism-workflow:load-plan: Load a previously saved plan or spec for the current project directory. This skill should be used when the user wants to load a plan, recall a spec, review a saved design document, or says "load-plan".
- minimalism-workflow:gh-issue-filer: This skill should be used when the user wants to file a GitHub issue, create a bug report, request a feature, report a problem, or open an issue in the current repository. It explores the codebase to understand project context, gathers issue details through conversation, and files the issue via the gh CLI. Trigger phrases include "file an issue", "create an issue", "report a bug", "request a feature", "open an issue", or "gh issue".
- minimalism-workflow:store-release-note: Generate concise App Store / Play Store release notes from the latest GitHub release. This skill should be used when the user wants to write app store release notes, play store changelog, or compress release notes for mobile store submission.
- minimalism-workflow:cleanup-plans: Clean up saved plans from the planvault. This skill should be used when the user wants to delete old plans, remove stale specs, prune the planvault, or says "cleanup-plans".
- minimalism-workflow:save-plan: Save a plan or spec as markdown, associated with the current project directory. This skill should be used when the user wants to save a plan, persist a spec, store a design document, or says "save-plan".
- minimalism-workflow:list-plans: List all saved plans from the planvault, optionally filtered by the current project. This skill should be used when the user wants to see available plans, browse saved specs, or says "list-plans".
- minimalism-workflow:release-tag: Create a git tag from the project's version file (package.json, Cargo.toml, pubspec.yaml, build.gradle, etc.). This skill should be used when the user wants to tag a release, create a version tag, or tag the current commit for release.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
## 요약

대화 참여자: kodingwarrior (VIM), Dalinaum, 홍민희

**맥락**
- 회사에서 룰베이스 코드리뷰 봇과 씨름 중 (예: `contextW`의 `W`가 지역 코드라 오해받는 등). CodeRabbit/Gemini가 낫다는 얘기.
- 이 프로젝트용으로 GitHub Actions에 린트 에러 체크 + PR 피드백을 붙이고 싶음. 워닝은 현재 너무 많아서 당장은 무시.

**기술 결론**
- `./gradlew lintDebug` 출력 중 `w:` / `e:` 접두어로 시작하는 줄만 파싱.
- 포맷: `w:|e: file:///path:line:col <message>` — 부가 설명(여러 줄)은 무시.
- 1단계: 워닝 포함해서 동작 검증 → 2단계: `e:`만 필터링해 PR 코멘트.
- `grep`으로 충분히 비벼볼 수 있다는 판단.
- 이후엔 ktlint도 같은 방식으로 붙일 예정.

## 다음 액션 아이템

1. **GitHub Actions 워크플로 추가**: `./gradlew lintDebug` 실행 → 출력에서 `^[we]:` 줄만 추출 → `file:line:col` 기반으로 PR에 리뷰 코멘트 달기.
2. **1차 검증**: 먼저 워닝 포함해 파이프라인 전체가 도는지 확인.
3. **2차 축소**: `e:`만 남기도록 필터 좁히고, 에러는 반드시 대응하도록 블로킹.
4. **후속**: 동일 패턴으로 ktlint 붙이기.

지금 바로 GitHub Actions 워크플로 초안을 만들까요?

file.content:
name: F-Droid Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  build-release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up JDK 17
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '17'
          distribution: 'temurin'

      - name: Setup Gradle
        uses: gradle/actions/setup-gradle@v4

      - name: Decode keystore
        env:
          KEYSTORE_BASE64: ${{ secrets.KEYSTORE_BASE64 }}
        run: echo "$KEYSTORE_BASE64" | base64 -d > ${{ github.workspace }}/release.keystore

      - name: Build signed release APK and App Bundle
        env:
          KEYSTORE_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/release.keystore
          KEY_ALIAS: ${{ secrets.KEY_ALIAS }}
          KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.KEY_PASSWORD }}
          STORE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.STORE_PASSWORD }}
        run: ./gradlew assembleRelease bundleRelease

      - name: Upload APK and App Bundle to GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          files: |
            app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk
            app/build/outputs/bundle/release/app-release.aab
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Clean up keystore
        if: always()
        run: rm -f ${{ github.workspace }}/release.keystore

content.text: Now I'll create the workflow. Strategy: run `./gradlew lintDebug`, parse `w:`/`e:` lines to GitHub annotations so they appear inline on PR diff, fail the job only on `e:`.

input.content:
name: Lint

on:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write
      checks: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up JDK 17
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '17'
          distribution: 'temurin'

      - name: Setup Gradle
        uses: gradle/actions/setup-gradle@v4

      - name: Run lintDebug
        id: lint
        run: |
          set -o pipefail
          ./gradlew lintDebug --console=plain 2>&1 | tee lint.log
        continue-on-error: true

      - name: Emit annotations
        run: |
          python3 - <<'PY'
          import os, re, sys

          workspace = os.environ.get("GITHUB_WORKSPACE", "")
          pattern = re.compile(
              r"^(?P<sev>[we]):\s+file://(?P<path>[^:]+):(?P<line>\d+):(?P<col>\d+)\s+(?P<msg>.*)$"
          )

          errors = 0
          warnings = 0
          with open("lint.log", "r", errors="replace") as f:
              for raw in f:
                  line = raw.rstrip("\n")
                  m = pattern.match(line)
                  if not m:
                      continue
                  path = m.group("path")
                  if workspace and path.startswith(workspace + "/"):
                      path = path[len(workspace) + 1:]
                  sev = "error" if m.group("sev") == "e" else "warning"
                  if sev == "error":
                      errors += 1
                  else:
                      warnings += 1
                  msg = m.group("msg").replace("%", "%25").replace("\r", "%0D").replace("\n", "%0A")
                  print(f"::{sev} file={path},line={m.group('line')},col={m.group('col')}::{msg}")

          print(f"::notice::Lint summary — errors: {errors}, warnings: {warnings}")
          sys.exit(1 if errors > 0 else 0)
          PY

      - name: Upload lint log
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: lint-log
          path: lint.log

content.text:
No — my draft runs `lintDebug` on the whole module and emits annotations for every `w:`/`e:` line.

Two options:

1. **Whole-module, filter annotations to changed files** (simple): run lint as-is, then use `git diff --name-only <base>...HEAD` to get PR-changed files and only emit annotations/fail for those. Existing issues in untouched files stay invisible to the PR.
2. **Whole-module, no filter**: report everything. Noisy since warnings are many.

Option 1 matches the intent ("워닝은 많아서 그대로 둬야 할 것 같아요" — don't block PRs on pre-existing noise). Want me to go with that?

input.content:
name: Lint

on:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
      checks: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Set up JDK 17
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '17'
          distribution: 'temurin'

      - name: Setup Gradle
        uses: gradle/actions/setup-gradle@v4

      - name: Run lintDebug
        run: ./gradlew lintDebug --console=plain 2>&1 | tee lint.log
        continue-on-error: true

      - name: Collect changed files
        id: changed
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
            BASE="${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}"
            HEAD="${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}"
            git diff --name-only "$BASE" "$HEAD" > changed.txt
          else
            git diff --name-only "${{ github.event.before }}" "${{ github.sha }}" > changed.txt || true
          fi
          echo "changed files:"
          cat changed.txt

      - name: Emit annotations
        run: |
          python3 - <<'PY'
          import os, re, sys

          workspace = os.environ.get("GITHUB_WORKSPACE", "")

          changed = set()
          try:
              with open("changed.txt") as f:
                  for line in f:
                      line = line.strip()
                      if line:
                          changed.add(line)
          except FileNotFoundError:
              pass

          pattern = re.compile(
              r"^(?P<sev>[we]):\s+file://(?P<path>[^:]+):(?P<line>\d+):(?P<col>\d+)\s+(?P<msg>.*)$"
          )

          errors = 0
          warnings = 0
          with open("lint.log", "r", errors="replace") as f:
              for raw in f:
                  line = raw.rstrip("\n")
                  m = pattern.match(line)
                  if not m:
                      continue
                  path = m.group("path")
                  if workspace and path.startswith(workspace + "/"):
                      path = path[len(workspace) + 1:]
                  if changed and path not in changed:
                      continue
                  sev = "error" if m.group("sev") == "e" else "warning"
                  if sev == "error":
                      errors += 1
                  else:
                      warnings += 1
                  msg = (
                      m.group("msg")
                      .replace("%", "%25")
                      .replace("\r", "%0D")
                      .replace("\n", "%0A")
                  )
                  print(f"::{sev} file={path},line={m.group('line')},col={m.group('col')}::{msg}")

          print(f"::notice::Lint (changed files) — errors: {errors}, warnings: {warnings}")
          sys.exit(1 if errors > 0 else 0)
          PY

      - name: Upload lint log
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: lint-log
          path: lint.log

content.text:
Created `.github/workflows/lint.yml`.

**What it does**
- Triggers on PRs and pushes to `main`.
- Runs `./gradlew lintDebug` (non-blocking via `continue-on-error`) and tees the output to `lint.log`.
- Computes changed files: `base...head` for PRs, `before...sha` for pushes.
- Parses `^[we]: file://<path>:<line>:<col> <msg>` lines, converts absolute paths to repo-relative, drops anything not in the changed set.
- Emits `::error`/`::warning` annotations so they land inline on the PR Files Changed view.
- Fails the job only if there's at least one `e:` in a changed file. Warnings surface but don't block.
- Uploads `lint.log` as an artifact for the full picture.

**Verification plan (phase 1 from the chat)**
Push to a throwaway branch, open a PR that touches a file with a known warning (e.g. `HtmlContent.kt:371`), confirm the annotation shows up on the diff. If the format looks right, phase 2 (error-only filter) is just flipping the parser regex to `^e:` — but with the current fail-on-error-only behavior, that may not even be needed.

Want me to also set it up for `ktlint`, or ship this first and iterate?

attachment.stdout: OK

content.text:
Three ways, cheapest to most realistic:

**1. Local dry-run of the parser** (fastest, no CI needed)
```
./gradlew lintDebug --console=plain 2>&1 | tee lint.log
git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD > changed.txt
# then run the same python block locally
```
Annotations print to stdout as `::warning file=...::...`. You can eyeball counts and paths without any PR.

**2. `act` — run the workflow locally**
```
brew install act
act pull_request -W .github/workflows/lint.yml
```
Full workflow execution in a Docker runner. Annotations show in terminal output. Caveat: JDK/Gradle cache setup on act is slow the first time.

**3. Draft PR on your fork** (most realistic)
- Branch off `main`, tweak a file that already has a known warning (e.g. `HtmlContent.kt` line 371 `ClickableText` deprecation) — even just adding a blank line is enough to mark it "changed".
- Push to `malkoG/hackerspub-android`, open a **draft PR** against `malkoG/main` (not upstream) so you don't spam upstream.
- Check the Files Changed tab for inline annotations and the Checks tab for the summary.

I'd suggest (1) first to verify the parser regex matches real output, then (3) to verify the PR-annotation plumbing. Want me to run step 1 now?

content.text:
Here's the concrete plan. Need your call on two things before I push.

**Plan**
1. Create branch `ci/add-lint-workflow` off `main`.
2. Commit `.github/workflows/lint.yml`.
3. *(optional)* Add a tiny change to a file with a known warning — e.g. a whitespace edit at `HtmlContent.kt:371` — so annotations actually light up on the diff. Without this, `changed files` is just the workflow file itself and you won't see any lint output.
4. Push to `origin` (`malkoG/hackerspub-android`).
5. Open a **draft PR**.

**Questions**
- **PR target**: open against `malkoG:main` for testing, or straight to `hackers-pub/android:main`? Your memory says real PRs go to upstream, but this is a throwaway experiment — `malkoG:main` is safer (no upstream noise, workflow still runs with full permissions since it's same-repo).
- **Trivial edit**: include the `HtmlContent.kt` whitespace touch in step 3, or keep the PR workflow-only? Without the touch the first run will be a no-op for annotations — useful to confirm the "no noise on unrelated files" path but not the "annotation appears inline" path.

My recommendation: **target `malkoG:main`, include the whitespace touch**. Shall I proceed?

gh pr create --draft --repo malkoG/hackerspub-android --base main --head ci/add-lint-workflow --title "Experiment: lint CI workflow with PR annotations" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Adds `.github/workflows/lint.yml`: runs `./gradlew lintDebug`, parses `w:`/`e:` lines from Kotlin compiler output, filters to files changed in this PR, and emits GitHub inline annotations.
- Job fails only on `e:` in changed files; warnings surface but don't block.
- Includes a throwaway reformat of `HtmlContent.kt` around line 371 so the known `ClickableText` deprecation warning sits inside the diff — lets us verify the annotation plumbing end-to-end.

## Test plan
- [ ] Confirm the `Lint` check runs on this PR.
- [ ] Confirm a `warning` annotation appears inline on `HtmlContent.kt` in the Files Changed view.
- [ ] Confirm no annotations appear for files outside the diff, even though lintDebug emits warnings project-wide.
- [ ] Confirm the `lint-log` artifact is uploaded.
- [ ] Once verified: revert the `HtmlContent.kt` reformat (or drop this PR entirely) and open a clean upstream PR with just the workflow file.
EOF
)"
